title: Pipeline Run Context
description: Manifest for a single pipeline run. See docs/context-json-guide.md. The boolean form of each review_gates entry is the Phase 1 baseline; the object form is reserved for the Phase 2 audit-field extension (oneOf accepts both, backward-compatible).

schema_version.description: Version of this schema the document conforms to. Phase 1 uses '1.0'.

run_id.description: Unique per-run identifier. Phase 1 convention: ISO-8601-ish timestamp + short hash, e.g. 2026-05-28T14-32-01Z-a1b2c3d.

id.description: Top of the traceability chain. STORY-XXX for manual mode, the Jira issue key for Jira mode.

path.description: Relative path to the local Markdown copy of the story.

jira_issue_key.description: Required when source == 'jira'. Equal to story.id in that case.

acceptance_criteria.description: One AC per element. The array index is the canonical position used by risks[].related_acs.

risks.description: First traceability anchor after the story. Each entry gets a RISK-XXX id.

code_change_context.description: OPTIONAL (Phase 3 TG15, Improvement 1). SECONDARY context only: the diff of a linked PR, used to sharpen regression scope and risk prioritization. It NEVER defines expected behavior — acceptance_criteria remain the source of truth (CLAUDE.md §3.8). Populated by the Analyst (Mode B) only when the Jira story has a linked PR; absent otherwise (the pipeline runs identically with no diff). Filled via the read-only GitHub MCP; no custom diff engine, no deploy/ECR infra.

linked_pr.description: URL of the linked pull request, e.g. https://github.com/org/repo/pull/123.

base_sha.description: The base/target SHA (the 'deployed' side). May be a configured branch like main.

related_risk_ids.description: Optional. RISK-XXX this changed file maps to, linking code change -> risk for regression prioritization.

summary.description: Short LLM summary of what the diff does, in the Analyst's own words. Secondary context, not a requirement.

fetched_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of when the diff was fetched.

prompt_versions.description: OPTIONAL (Phase 3 TG8, prompt versioning). Records which agent prompt VERSION drove this run, keyed by agent name (the frontmatter `name:` of agents/*.md), value = that file's `version:` (semver). This pins a run to the exact prompts that produced it, so a metrics/regression delta can be attributed to a specific prompt change. Absent on older runs (the pipeline runs identically without it). Keys are agent names; this object is NOT a closed enum so new agents can register without a schema change.

additionalProperties.description: Semantic version of the agent prompt used (matches agents/<name>.md frontmatter `version:`).

artifact_paths.description: Manifest of files this run produces. Empty string means 'not yet produced'.

review_gates.description: Phase 1 form uses booleans. Phase 2 (TG6) introduces an optional audit-field object alongside the boolean via oneOf; both forms remain valid. Phase 2 (TG7) adds the OPTIONAL qa_scope_approved gate that consolidates Gates 1 and 2 — see docs/review-gates.md. The four per-gate fields stay required for backward compatibility; qa_scope_approved is additive and never replaces code_reviewed (Gate 4 never consolidates).

qa_scope_approved.description: OPTIONAL (Phase 2 TG7). When present and passed, consolidates Gate 1 (requirements_reviewed) and Gate 2 (test_scope_reviewed) into a single approval. The two underlying gates remain present and valid (deprecated, not removed). Same boolean | audit-object shape as the other gates. Gate 4 (code_reviewed) is NEVER consolidated into this.

reviewed_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp, or null when not yet recorded.
